result.summary: Printful exposes REST HTTP APIs for its main API v1, API v2 beta, and Embedded Design Maker-related endpoints; authentication is via private bearer tokens, with public-app OAuth also documented for the main API. No GraphQL API, MCP server, or CLI was found.
result.description: Printful is a print-on-demand and dropshipping platform that lets merchants and creators sell custom products without holding inventory. It handles product printing, fulfillment, and shipping, and also offers enterprise design-maker capabilities.

private_token.label: Private OAuth access token
private_token.setup: Sign in to the [Printful Developers portal](https://developers.printful.com) using your Printful account, then in the `Tokens` section click `Add new token`. Set the token name, contact info, expiration date, access level (`Account (all stores)` or a single store), and select scopes. For Embedded Design Maker, create a token with the `Embedded Design Maker` extension enabled; the docs recommend separate development and production tokens. The docs note that scopes are fixed after creation.

public_app.label: Printful public app OAuth client
public_app.setup: Sign in to the [Printful Developers portal](https://developers.printful.com) and create a public app/project for distribution to other Printful users. The API docs describe `Private tokens and public apps`, `Client types`, `Scopes`, and `Public App authorization`; the developers login page shows Printful's OAuth authorization flow. Configure your app there and request the scopes your app needs.
